rulings.Case Title: DAVID SEWELL et al VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Dana Companies, Llc?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Siemens Industry, Inc.'s joinder in Dana Companies, LLC's motion to stay or dismiss action on grounds of forum non conveniens is off calendar at Siemens' request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SEWELL et al VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Dana Companies, Llcs Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc.'s joinder in Dana Companies, LLC's motion to stay or dismiss action on grounds of forum non conveniens is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to give proper notice required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and 1010.6. The denial is without prejudice to defendant filing its own properly-noticed motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Shell Oil Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their causes of action alleged against Shell. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant FMC Technologies, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)
